hawkish_phrases: inflationary pressures in the United Kingdom and the rest of Europe have intensified significantly
There is a risk that a longer period of externally generated price inflation will lead to more enduring domestic price and wage pressures
The Committee will be particularly alert to indications of more persistent inflationary pressures, and will if necessary act forcefully in response
a more forceful policy action was justified
CPI inflation is expected to rise more than forecast in the May Report, from 9.4% in June to just over 13% in 2022 Q4
dovish_phrases: The United Kingdom is now projected to enter recession from the fourth quarter of this year
Real household post-tax income is projected to fall sharply in 2022 and 2023, while consumption growth turns negative
GDP growth in the United Kingdom is slowing
One member preferred a 0.25 percentage point increase in Bank Rate at this meeting
Bank Rate might already have reached the level consistent with returning inflation to the 2% target in the medium term
summary: The MPC voted 8-1 to raise Bank Rate by 50bp to 1.75%, with the majority favoring forceful action against intensifying inflationary pressures and explicitly signaling willingness to act forcefully if persistent pressures emerge, despite projecting an imminent recession.
